description: This lecture discusses the limitations of programming on the Solana blockchain, focusing on stack size and compute unit restrictions. It includes a demonstration of building a simple marketplace application, highlighting the differences between two struct implementations: Marketplace Borsch and Marketplace. The speaker explains the implications of these limitations on application design and performance.
topic_summary: Overview of Solana's programming constraints, including stack size limitations [t=210.36], compute unit restrictions [t=252.93], and account allocation limits [t=310.86]. Demonstration of building a marketplace application with practical examples.
